concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Dizzy Gillespie, J.J. Johnson, and Sun Ra

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: I used to go see A.T., when A.T. was in the band playing, Arthur Taylor. I used to go and sit with him, because we were tight... But we was – we used to hang out together. We used to talk a lot.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: The record was with Art Taylor and Gene Ramey and they played beautifully for both of us
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: We had a band with Kenny Dorham and Art Taylor and Gene Ramey.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Donaldson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Buster Williams
about_quotes.evidence: The way Art Blakey plays the drums, the way Elvin Jones played the drums, the way Philly Jo Jones played the drums, the way Art Taylor played the drums. Everybody had their own sound.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buster Williams interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: this music's so Arthur Taylor bought a brand new automobile, he couldn't drive a lick, no lick, no license, so he took this car, somebody drove it over there to New Jersey.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: The 21st of June, I did a concert in Joinville, a suburb of Paris with Benny Carter, Ben Webster, Kenny Drew, Art Taylor, Michel Sarbadi, Johnny Griffin...
